欢迎回来，机芯用户们。距离Calibre4.0已经过去一年了。这两个标题特性突出了CalibreE-book查看器中的支持，而Calibre现在已经移到了Python3。
已经有很多关于口径电子书阅读器的工作。它现在支持高亮显示。突出显示可以是颜色、下划线、删除线等，并添加了注释。所有亮点都可以存储在EPUB文件中以便于共享，也可以集中在Calibre库中以便于浏览。此外，电子书查看器现在支持竖排和从右向左的文本。
Calibre已经改用Python3，这是因为Python2已经在今年停用了。这对Calibre用户来说应该是完全透明的，唯一的警告是一些第三方Calibre插件还没有导入到Python3中，因此不能在Calibre 5中工作。有关各种插件端口的状态，请参见此处。这项工作包括将50万行Python代码和数万行扩展代码移植到Python3，如果没有Eli Schwartz和Flaviu Tamas的帮助，这是不可能的。
现在，您可以在电子书查看器中突出显示文本。可以创建多种样式的高亮显示以及关联的注释。默认情况下，高亮显示EPUB书本时，高亮显示会存储在书本文件本身中，以便于共享。它们也集中存储在Calibre库中，可以使用浏览注释工具进行浏览，您可以通过右键单击查看按钮来访问该工具。
高亮显示甚至可以使用作为Calibre内容服务器一部分的浏览器内查看器来工作。您可以通过电子书查看器首选项的其他部分中的设置，选择使在那里创建的亮点与在Calibre电子书查看器中创建的亮点自动同步。
Calibre现在在主界面、电子书查看器、电子书编辑器甚至内容服务器中完全支持黑暗模式。在Windows和MacOS上，暗模式会根据操作系统设置自动激活。在Linux上，您必须设置CALIBRE_USE_DUSE_DUSE_PALET=1环境变量。
电子书查看器现在支持全字、正则表达式等增强的搜索。搜索结果按章节分组显示。搜索完成后，有一个按钮可以将您带回书中的位置。
由于Calibre现在已经迁移到Python3，一些第三方插件将不再工作，直到它们也被移植到Python3。有关插件移植工作的状态，请参见此处。
由于支持库范围的注释，因此数据库格式稍有更改。虽然Calibre 4.23能够打开并操作Calibre 5数据库，但较旧版本的Calibre可能会遇到问题。
这是一个恰当的时机，我们要感谢CalibreCommunity社区，他们无私地贡献了他们的精力和热情--如果没有他们，上面的许多功能就永远不会出现在人们的视线中。(工业和信息化部电子科学技术情报研究所陈皓)。
请注意，其中一些功能实际上是在4.x系列的生命周期中引入的。本文档介绍了与4.0相比的新功能。
查看之前主要版本中的新特性：4.0、3.0、2.0、1.0、0.9、0.8、0.7。
note_en:
Welcome back, calibre users. It has been a year since calibre4.0. The two headline features are  Highlighting support in the calibreE-book viewer and that calibre has now moved to  Python 3.
 There has been a lot of work on the calibre E-book viewer. Itnow supports  Highlighting. The highlights can be colors, underlines,strikethrough, etc. and have added notes. All highlights can be both stored inEPUB files for easy sharing and centrally in the calibre library for easy browsing.Additionally, the E-book viewer now supports both vertical and right-to-left text.
 calibre has moved to using Python 3. This is because Python 2was end-of-lifed this year. This should be completely transparent to calibre users,the only caveat being that some third party calibre plugins have not yet been portedto Python 3 and therefore will not work in calibre 5. For status on the variousplugin ports,  see here. This effort involved porting half-a-millionlines of Python code and tens-of-thousands of lines of extension code to Python 3. Thiswould not have been possible without the help of Eli Schwartz and Flaviu Tamas.
 You can now highlight text in the E-book viewer. Highlights can be created in many styles, with associated notes. By default, when highlighting EPUB books, the highlights are stored in the book file itself for easy sharing. They are also stored centrally in the calibre library and can be browsed using the  Browse annotations tool that you can access by right clicking the  View button.
    Highlighting even works using the in-browser viewer that is part of the calibre Content server. You can choose to have highlights created there sync automatically with highlights created in the calibre E-book viewer via a setting in the  Miscellaneous section of the E-book viewer preferences.
  calibre now fully supports dark mode throughout, in the main interface, the E-book viewer, the E-book editor and even the Content Server. On Windows and macOS dark mode is activated automatically based on operating system settings. On Linux you have to set the  CALIBRE_USE_DARK_PALETTE=1 environment variable.
 The E-book viewer now supports enhanced searches with whole word, regular expressions, etc. Search results are displayed grouped by chapter. There is a button that takes you back to where in the book you were after you are done searching.
  Because calibre has now moved to Python 3, some third party plugins will no longer work, until they are also ported to Python 3. For status on the plugin porting effort,  see here.
  Because of the support for library wide annotations, the database format was slightly changed. While calibre 4.23 is able to open and operate with calibre 5 databases, older versions of calibre may have trouble.
  This is an appropriate time to throw out a big thank you to the calibrecommunity who have contributed selflessly of their energy and enthusiasm —without which many of the features above would never have seen the light ofday.
 Note that some of these features were actually introduced during thelifetime of the 4.x series. This document describes new features as comparedto 4.0
 See what was new in previous major calibre releases: 4.0,  3.0,  2.0,  1.0,  0.9,  0.8,  0.7.
title: 发布了Calibre 5.0

note:
11岁的男孩在给大卫·卡梅伦写信并谈论《权力的游戏》中的斩首事件后，被错误地怀疑为未来的恐怖分子，他赢得了与大都会歌剧院的法律诉讼，要求删除他的数据。
一名11岁的男孩在给大卫·卡梅伦写了一封信，并在电视剧“权力的游戏”中谈论斩首事件后，被错误地怀疑为未来的恐怖分子。他赢得了一场高等法院与伦敦警察局的斗争，要求删除他的个人数据。
2015年，在一名导师错误地表示担心他痴迷于杀害首相后，这名男孩(只被称为II)被报告给了政府领导的反激进主义组织Prepect。
预防官员总结说，在2016年6月结案之前，没有激进化/反恐方面的担忧，导师的担忧似乎是一种误解。
但在2019年4月，大都会博物馆决定将男孩的个人信息保留在10个独立的数据库中，其中一些数据库可以被内政部官员和几个地方当局以及反恐官员访问，至少到2022年。
现在，高等法院的一名法官裁定，将男孩的数据存档并不是出于治安目的。
防范官员采访了男孩的母亲，她说她的儿子最近写了一封信，他想寄给卡梅伦先生，信中谈到了全世界的和平与团结。
她还说，她不让他看“权力的游戏”，这样他就不会知道该剧中的暴力行为。她还告诉警方，你们可能会把“权力的游戏”视为未来的恐怖分子，这让我感到恐惧和难过。(译者注：“权力的游戏”是“权力的游戏”。)她还说，她不让他看“权力的游戏”，这样他就不会知道该剧中的暴力行为。她还告诉警方，这让我感到恐惧和悲哀。
这名男孩来自伦敦东部，现在16岁，他对大都会博物馆采取了法律行动，声称保留他的个人数据侵犯了他的隐私权，违反了数据保护法。
在一份目击者陈述中，他的母亲说，II是一个聪明的年轻人，计划申请英国的一所顶尖大学，并接受成为一名医生的培训。
她补充说：他担心在未来的某个时候，不真实的数据可能会影响任何潜在的警方记录搜索，并可能导致对他的进一步监控，并危及他未来的前景。
大都会表示，保留II&39；的个人数据对他的影响微乎其微，而且不可能与任何教育机构或任何未来的雇主共享。
但II&39；的律师指出，不能保证这些数据不会泄露给第三方。
在周四发表的一份判决书中，斯泰恩法官发现，没有证据表明继续持有原告的个人数据是出于治安目的，保留这些数据违反了II；的隐私权和“数据保护法”(Data Protection Act)。
法官表示，大都会博物馆低估了对原告隐私权的干扰的影响，这些隐私权需要保留他11岁时所谓的观点和声明的数据。
她补充说：仅保留数据就意味着(大都会警察局)警官、全国反恐官员、地方当局和内政部同事可以访问10个数据库中的数据。
斯泰恩法官还表示，不能保证索赔人的个人数据不会泄露给第三方。她说，这让一名16岁的男孩担心自己可能会被错误地贴上恐怖主义支持者的标签。
在裁决后的一份声明中，II&#39；的母亲说：在过去的五年里，乌云一直笼罩着我的家人。
我儿子对他的指控严重误导，可能会毁了他的未来。
经常活着，不知道关于你孩子的虚假信息(公共机构可以接触到)是否会被分享，阻碍他在一个你一直知道是家乡的国家自由生活的机会，是令人心碎的。
对我们来说，这片乌云消散了，因为我们知道在哪里、向谁寻求建议。我们得到了一支出色的法律团队的指导--但那些不知道的人怎么办？
迪顿·皮尔斯·格林律师事务所(Deighton Piels Glynn)的律师巴林·卡尔西(Bharine Kalsi)表示：法院今天发现，没有任何治安目的是为了保留有关II数据的数据，这些数据暗示着激进主义，但我们的客户一直坚称这些数据是不真实的。
今天的判断意味着，这一数据将不再给II的光明未来蒙上令人担忧的阴影，特别是因为不能保证不会与其他组织和机构分享。
现在是当局根据预防战略来审视他们行使权力的时候了，该战略继续不公正地针对无辜的个人，特别是来自穆斯林社区的儿童。
note_en:
Boy, 11, who was wrongly suspected of being a future terrorist after writing letter to David Cameron and talking about beheadings in Game of Thrones wins legal battle against Met to have his data deleted
         A boy of 11 who was wrongly suspected of being a future terrorist after writing a letter to David Cameron and talking about beheadings in the TV show Game of Thrones has won a High Court battle against the Metropolitan Police to have his personal data deleted.
 The boy - known only as II - was reported to government-led anti-radicalistation scheme Prevent in 2015 after a tutor raised 'misinformed' concerns in that he was 'obsessed' with killing the prime minister.
 Prevent officers concluded 'there were no radicalisation/counter-terrorism concerns' and the tutor's fears 'appeared to be a misunderstanding' before closing the case in June 2016.
 But in April 2019, the Met decided to retain the boy's personal information on 10 separate databases, some of which can be accessed by Home Office officials and several local authorities as well as counter-terrorism officers, until at least 2022.
 Now a High Court judge has ruled there is 'no policing purpose' for keeping the boy's data on file.
  Prevent officers spoke to the boy's mother who said her son had recently written a letter he wanted to send to Mr Cameron which 'spoke of peace and unity throughout the world'.
  She also said she 'did not let him watch Game Of Thrones so he wouldn't know anything about the violence in the show', and told police it was 'frightening and saddening to me that you are potentially viewing II as a possible terrorist in the future'.
 The boy - who is from east London and is now 16 - took legal action against the Met, claiming the retention of his personal data was a breach of his right to privacy and a breach of the Data Protection Act.
 In a witness statement, his mother said that II is 'a bright young man, with plans to apply to a top university in the UK and to train to become a medical doctor'.
 She added: 'He is afraid that at some point in the future the untrue data may affect any potential police record searches and could give rise to further monitoring of him, and jeopardise his future prospects.'
 The Met said the retention of II's personal data would have a 'minimal impact' on him, and there was 'no way' it would be shared with 'any educational institution or any prospective employer'.
  But II's lawyers pointed out there had been 'no guarantee that the data will not be disclosed to third parties'.
  In a judgment delivered on Thursday, Mrs Justice Steyn found that 'no policing purpose for continuing to hold the claimant's personal data has been demonstrated' and that its retention breached II's right to privacy and the Data Protection Act.
 The judge said the Met had 'underestimated the impact of the interference with the claimant's privacy rights entailed in retaining data about his alleged views and statements when he was 11 years old'.
 She added: 'Retention alone means that the data can be accessed by (Metropolitan Police Service) officers, counter-terrorism officers nationally, local authorities and Home Office colleagues, across 10 databases.'
 Mrs Justice Steyn also said there was 'no guarantee that the claimant's personal data will not be disclosed to third parties', which she said 'engenders fear in a 16-year-old boy that he may be tagged - wrongly - as a supporter of terrorism'.
 In a statement after the ruling, II's mother said: 'A dark cloud had loomed over my family for the past five years.
 'My son had grossly misinformed allegations cast against him that could have had the potential to destroy his future.
 'To constantly live, not knowing whether false information about your child, accessible by public bodies, would be shared, hampering his chance to live freely in a country you have always known to be home, is beyond heart-breaking.
 'For us, this cloud passed, because we knew where and who to seek advice from. We were guided by a brilliant legal team - but what about those who don't know?'
 II's solicitor Bharine Kalsi, of Deighton Pierce Glynn, said: 'The court today has found that there was 'no policing purpose' for the retention of data concerning II - data which alludes to radicalisation, but which our client has always maintained is untrue.
 'Today's judgment means this data will no longer cast a worrying shadow over II's bright future, particularly as there is no guarantee that it would not be shared with other organisations and institutions.
 'It is now time for the authorities to look at their exercise of power under the Prevent strategy, which continues to unjustly target innocent individuals, in particular children from the Muslim community.'
title: 11岁的男孩被错误地怀疑为未来的恐怖分子，他赢得了法律诉讼
title_en: Boy, 11, who was wrongly suspected of being a future terrorist wins legal battle
